---
title: |
  Materials Genomics<br>Unit 13: Reproducibility, Deployment, and MLOps for Materials Labs
bibliography: ref.bib
author:
  - name: Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz
    affiliation:
      - FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg
execute:
  eval: false
format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    css: custom.css
    theme: custom.scss
    slide-number: c/t
    logo: "eclipse_logo_small.png"
    footer: "© Philipp Pelz - Materials Genomics"
---

## 01. Title: Reproducibility, Deployment, and MLOps for Materials Labs
- Unit scope and capstone role.

## 02. Learning outcomes
- Operationalize MG pipelines responsibly.

## 03. Recap from Units 1–12
- From data to candidate generation.

## 04. Why MLOps in materials labs
- Reliability, traceability, and scale.

## 05. Reproducibility foundations
- Data, code, environment versioning.

## 06. Data lineage and provenance
- Source-to-result traceability.

## 07. Experiment tracking systems
- Runs, metrics, artifacts.

## 08. Model registry concept
- Versioned model lifecycle.

## 09. Pipeline orchestration overview
- ETL, training, validation, deployment.

## 10. CI/CD for scientific ML
- Automated checks and gates.

## 11. Validation before deployment
- Holdout, stress, and safety tests.

## 12. Monitoring after deployment
- Drift, calibration, and KPI health.

## 13. Retraining triggers
- Data shift and performance decay.

## 14. Batch vs online deployment
- Operational tradeoffs.

## 15. Human-in-the-loop review
- Decision support over full autonomy.

## 16. Governance and accountability
- Roles, approvals, and audits.

## 17. Documentation standards
- Model cards + datasheets.

## 18. Uncertainty in operations
- Confidence-gated actions.

## 19. Explainability in deployment
- Stakeholder communication needs.

## 20. Security and access control
- Protect data and model assets.

## 21. Compliance and ethics context
- Responsible deployment constraints.

## 22. Cost/performance optimization
- Compute budgets and latency.

## 23. Multi-fidelity production loops
- Integrating simulation + lab updates.

## 24. Closed-loop integration
- Candidate suggestion to experiment outcomes.

## 25. Incident response playbooks
- Failure detection and rollback.

## 26. Failure mode #1
- Silent data drift.

## 27. Failure mode #2
- Irreproducible pipeline changes.

## 28. Failure mode #3
- Overtrust in uncalibrated models.

## 29. Mitigation checklist
- Practical MLOps guardrails.

## 30. Case sketch: alloy deployment pipeline
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 31. Case sketch: polymer screening service
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 32. Case sketch: energy-materials prioritization
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 33. KPI dashboard design
- Scientific + operational metrics.

## 34. Team workflows
- Collaboration between ML and domain experts.

## 35. Reproducibility maturity model
- Stages for lab adoption.

## 36. Scaling roadmap
- From pilot to routine operation.

## 37. Capstone synthesis
- Connect all course units.

## 38. Exercise scaffold: define MLOps blueprint
- Architecture + governance.

## 39. Exercise scaffold: reproducibility audit
- Identify and close gaps.

## 40. Exercise scaffold: monitoring plan
- Drift and calibration alerts.

## 41. Exercise scaffold: incident simulation
- Rollback and recovery.

## 42. Exercise scaffold: deployment report
- Risks, controls, and KPIs.

## 43. Exam-oriented key statements
- High-yield operational concepts.

## 44. Summary table
- Components and responsibilities.

## 45. Unit summary
- Core takeaways.

## 46. References
- Source list.

## 47. Course closure perspective
- Practical roadmap after semester.

## 48. Q&A buffer slide
- Discussion and synthesis.

## 49. Final checklist slide
- What students should be able to do.

## 50. Thank-you / outlook
- Future directions.

::: {#refs}
:::
